question.text: Under the SOP for default by a TM or CM, SEBI requires exchanges to settle the credit balances of which category of investors as an interim measure?

answer.text: Small investors with balances less than Rs. 25 lakh are settled first using unencumbered deposits; investors with balances exceeding Rs. 25 lakh are paid on a pro-rata basis
All investors are settled equally regardless of balance size
Only institutional investors are settled first since they have larger exposures
No interim settlement is made; all claims go through the normal arbitration process

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In case of TM/CM default, exchanges must first settle small investors (< Rs. 25 lakh balance) using unencumbered deposits. Larger investors (> Rs. 25 lakh) are paid pro-rata from remaining funds. This prioritizes retail investor protection. **The Trap:** Small investors (< Rs. 25 lakh) get priority, not larger investors. The threshold is Rs. 25 lakh. **Cross-Exam Context:** Investor protection in default scenarios is tested in VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Default SOP: < Rs. 25L settled first from unencumbered deposits. > Rs. 25L settled pro-rata from remaining.
